symptoms: V-shaped yellow lesions on leaf margins
management: Hot water seed treatment (50°C for 30 mins); 3-year crop rotation; avoid overhead irrigation.
Remove bottom leaves; spray Carbendazim; keep foliage dry during evening hours.

symptoms: yellow translucent 'oil spots' on upper leaf

management: Use resistant rootstocks; maintain soil pH above 6.5; avoid wounding roots during transplanting.

management: Mulch to prevent soil splash; spray Copper-based fungicides.

symptoms: black hard masses on tuber skin (cannot be washed off)
management: Seed treatment with Pencycuron; avoid deep planting; harvest immediately after vine kill.

symptoms: corky brown lesions on tuber surface
management: Maintain high soil moisture during tuber initiation; lower soil pH to 5.2; avoid fresh manure.

management: Store in well-ventilated cool areas; avoid mechanical injury during harvest; dry tubers before storage.

symptoms: blackening of stem from tip downwards
management: Prune 2 inches below infected area; apply Bordeaux paste to cut ends.
Spray Carbendazim; avoid overhead irrigation during flowering.

symptoms: collapse of seedlings at soil level
management: Seed treatment with Thiram; avoid excess moisture in nursery beds.
Use disease-free cuttings; soil sterilization with steam or formalin.

Spray Copper Oxychloride; ensure balanced fertilization.
Increase air movement; drench with Metalaxyl; isolate infected plants.

management: Control sap-sucking insects (aphids/whiteflies) using starch spray or insecticides.

Apply Sodium or Ammonium Molybdate to soil; check soil pH.

symptoms: transformation of spikes into leafy structures
white cottony growth on leaf underside
management: Seed treatment with Metalaxyl (6g/kg); rogue out 'green ear' plants; use resistant hybrids like HHB 67.

management: Brine treatment (10% salt) to float off sclerotia; spray Mancozeb at 50% flowering; deep summer plowing.

management: Harvest promptly after physiological maturity; use cultivars with tan plant color and hard grain luster.

symptoms: individual grains replaced by gray-black fungal galls
management: Seed treatment with Carboxin or Thiram (2g/kg); crop rotation; removal of smutted ear heads.

management: Spray Wettable Sulfur (0.2%) or Mancozeb; destroy collateral hosts like 'Ischaemum aristatum'.

symptoms: spots coalesce to form large blighted areas
management: Seed treatment with Captan; spray Mancozeb (0.2%) if symptoms appear at seedling stage.

symptoms: vines wilt from tip to base
